text.title: DDD Aggregates Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed when implementing aggregates. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Caller does `order.items.append(new_item)` directly. Aggregate cannot enforce invariants (`max items`, `total recalculation`, `event raising`).
antipattern.root-cause: Child collection exposed as mutable; root command method missing.
antipattern.fix: Expose `items` as read-only (`@property` returning a tuple/frozenset). Add `order.add_item(...)` on the root that mutates the list, recalculates totals, and raises `OrderItemAdded`.

antipattern.symptom: `Customer` aggregate owns orders, addresses, payments, subscriptions. Loading a customer pulls thousands of rows; updates lock everything.
antipattern.root-cause: Author packed everything into one aggregate to "be safe".
antipattern.fix: Split along invariant boundaries: `Customer` (identity + profile), `Order` (its own aggregate referencing customer by ID), `Subscription`. Cross-aggregate consistency becomes eventual via domain events.

antipattern.symptom: `Order` has `self.customer = Customer(...)`. Loading an order eager-loads the full customer; saving touches both rows; merge conflicts when two transactions touch different aggregates.
antipattern.root-cause: Author preferred object reference to UUID, treating aggregates like in-memory graphs.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `self.customer_id: UUID`. If the application needs both, the application service loads them separately and coordinates.

antipattern.symptom: `OrderService.place_order(order)` validates `order.total > 0` before persisting. Same invariant duplicated in multiple services; one service forgets it; bad data reaches the DB.
antipattern.root-cause: Invariants placed in service layer; aggregate became a data bag.
antipattern.fix: Move the invariant onto the aggregate's command method (`order.place(...)`). Service becomes a thin coordinator dispatching the command.
